name: NXP Semiconductors N.V.
window: Q1 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 28 → Jun 22, 2026

events.text: Q1 FY26 (qtr. ending Mar-29): revenue $3.18bn (+12% YoY, -5% QoQ), $31m above the midpoint; non-GAAP GM 57.1%, op margin 33%, EPS $3.05. Auto ~$1.78bn +6% YoY (content/SDV, not units); I&IoT ~$628m +24% YoY; Mobile ~$391m +16%; Comm Infra ~$380m +21%. Data center >$500m guided for 2026 (vs ~$200m in 2025). Q2 guide: revenue $3.45bn (+18% YoY), GM 58%, EPS $3.50.
GS framed strong analog results as positive for the group including NXPI, reading NXP positively in the analog recovery alongside TXN/ADI/ON (briefing).
JPM groups NXPI into its OW semi baskets (briefing).

At JPM's TMC conference, NXP guided to double-digit-% growth in CY26 and CY27 as the order book builds (auto/industrial/data-center drivers), and explicitly said there has been no major restocking activity yet — a tailwind still to come ("Conf feedback").
NXP appeared on Redburn's 12m relative-high list alongside the broader semi/analog complex — stretched positioning (briefing).

NXP announced July price increases alongside TI and Infineon; Jefferies groups NXP (auto MCUs) among the analog franchises structurally insulated from Chinese indigenization, which targets only high-growth categories (SiC/GaN/GPUs), not MCUs/MOSFETs/analog ("Analog Semis").

German Tier-1 procurement expert flagged NXP as a clear share gainer: NXP and Infineon "doing well across the whole pipeline" (vs Renesas more susceptible), NXP with "the most cost-competitive products" and the most aggressive pricing; in auto analog ICs NXP has "much better traction than the others." Sees analog IC demand +10-15% YoY in Q2'26, book-to-bill 1.1-1.2, CY2026 demand +15-20%.

Expert judged NXP's gains to be more pricing/supply-driven (less durable) than design-in lock, and flagged ICs and MOSFETs as the families most constrained on material availability; lead times back to ~8 weeks standard, channel inventory now lean/just-in-time (Expert call).
Citi notes NXPI joining IFX/TXN in announcing a second round of price increases effective Jun/Jul, and that NXPI's fab utilization recovered toward the optimal mid-80s% ("800V power").

Primer frames NXP as the margin-resilience standout of the set: FY25 EBIT 24.8% (down only ~275bps from the FY22 peak) and ROIC 11.6%, on a higher-value auto-MCU mix. However it quantifies auto-MCU share loss to IFX (IFX share rose from 10% in 2019 to 36% in 2025, "mainly at the expense of Renesas and NXP"); NXP is a 10% partner in the EUR 10bn ESMC Dresden JV ("IFX Primer").
